sources_exist.note: Nine distinct URLs were audited across 11 occurrences. The exact Zweig URL and the legacy Ohio DOT URL did not resolve to accessible cited content; the other URLs resolved.

figures_match_sources.note: Deltek's 53%/38% adoption series, the 17%-to-71% BIM endpoints, Monograph's pricing mix, the FAR audit mechanism, and ACEC's automation survey are supported. The ACEC page does not report 114 platforms, the current Auxo page does not contain the quoted 4x-6x/5x-7x or 8x-11x/10x-13x ranges, and the BIM page does not report the asserted 49% 2009 point.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Role fractions are bounded and mostly consistent with task evidence, but current_adoption_pct=0.40 is an undocumented discount from a broad A&E 'leveraging tools' headline, t50=1.5 uses an unsupported 2009 BIM point, owners_60plus_pct=0.35 is an estimate from 50+ data, and pi_dist assumes an unsourced one-third public-revenue share.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic dataset inputs and displayed formulas follow the frozen rubric, and the PRESSURED terminal classification is consistent with the cap rules. However, B and M rely on unsupported consolidator and multiple claims, while A and C depend on inadequately evidenced transformations, so the rubric inputs are not validly established.

cross_checks_honest.note: The cross-checks explicitly disclose the very large potential uplift, separate durable demand from shrinking billable hours, keep PE evidence out of V and A, and describe audited-overhead, hourly, and competitive fixed-fee deflation channels.

source_audits.note: The ACEC article reports PE acquisitions rising from 12% in 2016 to 39% in 2023 and PE backing among ENR Top 100 firms rising from 4% to 22%; it does not report 114 PE-backed platforms or the quoted 2025 deal statistics.

The current guide discusses 3.5x-5.5x adjusted EBITDA for smaller founder-led firms and 5.5x-7.5x for established lower-middle-market firms, not the record's quoted 4x-6x and 5x-7x statements. Although 5.0x lies in a current directional range, the cited quotation and claimed midpoint basis do not match.

Deltek's release reports 53% of A&E firms leveraging AI tools, up from 38%, with listed uses in proposals, project planning, business development, and analytics. The 40% stricter-material-use value is transparently labeled as a judgmental discount rather than a sourced statistic.

The current guide gives platform-quality engineering businesses an 8.5x-12.0x+ directional range, but it does not contain the quoted 8x-11x and 10x-13x+ statements or establish that 10.5x is the bottom of a premium-scale band.

The release supports BIM adoption of 17% in 2007 and 71% in 2012 but does not provide the asserted 49% in 2009 point. The t50 derivation specifically relies on a 28%-to-49% two-year step that this citation does not establish.

The exact URL did not resolve to accessible content. Search results locate a moved page under /blogs/the-zweig-letter/ that supports 82% over age 50, 2% age 39 or younger, and retirement age 65, but neither the exact citation nor that moved page directly supports a 35% age-60-plus share.

The article explains that A/E firms calculate audited overhead rates under FAR Part 31 and that most state DOT pricing rules incorporate federal or similar rules, supporting the audited-cost mechanism in pi_dist.
The legacy Ohio DOT URL did not resolve to accessible guidance, so it cannot independently support the claimed overhead-multiplier and productivity-clawback mechanism.
The page reports lump-sum/fixed fee at 59%, 76% for 1-20-person firms, T&M at 23%, and not-to-exceed T&M at 12%, directly supporting the pricing mix used in pi_dist.

The ACEC report documents legal-liability, regulatory, contractual, validation, and safety-critical constraints on AI, supporting a material professional-liability moat, though it does not itself state the record's every-state licensure and firm-registration claim.

Monograph directly supports the 59% fixed-fee share, 76% fixed-fee share for 1-20-person firms, and the remaining T&M categories. It does not support the separate unsourced assertion that roughly one-third of industry revenue is public work.

reasons: Replace inputs.active_consolidators.url or enumerate recent PE-backed engineering platforms from an auditable source; https://www.acec.org/news/last-word-blog/post/private-equitys-power-play/ does not support inputs.active_consolidators.value=114.

Re-source inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult from transaction evidence whose published ranges match the claimed quotations and normalized-EBITDA size bands; the current https://auxocapitaladvisors.com/engineering-firm-valuation-multiples/ page contains different directional ranges.

Re-source inputs.historical_analogs and inputs.t50_years with a source that directly reports the 2009 BIM adoption point used in the two-year diffusion calculation; https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/bim-adoption-expands-from-17-in-2007-to-over-70-in-2012-according-to-new-mcgraw-hill-construction-report-189185591.html supports only the 2007 and 2012 endpoints.

Replace the dead inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url and directly substantiate the 60-plus share rather than inferring 35% from a 50-plus statistic; the exact https://zweiggroup.com/blogs/news/diversity-of-age URL did not resolve.

Replace inputs.pi_dist.sources[1] with a live primary FAR/AASHTO or DOT document and source the public-sector revenue weight used to set pi_dist=0.45; the exact Ohio DOT URL is inaccessible and the record gives no auditable basis for the roughly one-third weight.
